content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 8, 1990


Danny Walsh
33 Payne River Circle
Sacramento, CA 95831

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-281

Dear Mr. Walsh:

This letter is in response to your request for confirmation
of oral advice provided to you when you visited this office on
April 10, 1990, concerning your duties and responsibilities under
the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ We do
not provide advice concerning provisions of law other than the
Act. You may wish to consult with the Attorney General's office
regarding other provisions of law such as Public Contract Code
Section 10411.

QUESTION

Do the provisions of the Act prevent you from contracting
with the Air Resources Board to act as the facilitator for the
Santa Monica Bay restoration project?

CONCLUSION

The provisions of the Act do not prevent you from contracting
with the Air Resources Board to act as the facilitator for the
Santa Monica Bay restoration project.

FACTS

You are a former member of the Water Resources Control Board.
Your term expired in February of 1990. As a member of that board,
you served as chairman of the Santa Monica Bay restoration
project. You are now a private consultant. The Air Resources
Board would like to contract with you to act as the facilitator
for the Santa Monica Bay restoration project. The Santa Monica

_______________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

File No. A-90-281
Page 2

Bay restoration project is a joint federal and state effort
designed to assist with the cleanup of Santa Monica Bay. The
funds to pay for your services would come from the Air Resources
Board's budget. The Water Resources Control Board and the Air
Resources Board are separate state agencies.

ANALYSIS

The Act places certain restrictions upon "state administrative officials" who leave their position with the state. "State
administrative official" means every member, officer, employee or
consultant of a state administrative agency who as part of his or
her official responsibilities engages in any judicial, quasi-
judicial or other proceeding in other than a purely clerical,
secretarial or ministerial capacity. (Section 87400(b).) As a
member of the Water Resources Control Board and chairman of the
Santa Monica Bay restoration project, you were a state administra-
tive official subject to the provisions of the Act. (Id.)

Sections 87401 and 87402 provide:

Section 87401

No former state administrative official, after
the termination of his or her employment or term of
office, shall for compensation act as agent or at-
torney for, or otherwise represent, any other
person (other than the State of California) before
any court or state administrative agency or any
officer or employee thereof by making any formal or
informal appearance, or by making any oral or writ-
ten communication with the intent to influence, in
connection with any judicial, quasi-judicial or
other proceeding if both of the following apply:

(a) The State of California is a party or has
a direct and substantial interest.

(b) The proceeding is one in which the former
state administrative official participated.

Section 87402

No former state administrative official, after
the termination of his or her employment or term of
office shall for compensation aid, advise, counsel,
consult or assist in representing any other person
(except the State of California) in any proceeding
in which the official would be prohibited from ap-
pearing under Section 87401.

File No. A-90-281
Page 3


Sections 87401 and 87402 prohibit you from representing, aid-
ing, advising, counseling, consulting, or assisting in represent-
ing any person,2 for compensation, in connection with any proceed-
ing in which you participated as a member of the Water Resources
Control Board. However, Sections 87401 and 87402 do not prevent
you from contracting with the Air Resources Board or any other
state agency, whether or not the contract would involve matters on
which you worked as a state employee. Therefore, under the
provisions of the Act, you may seek employment as a consultant and
represent the interests of the Air Resources Board in matters
related to the Santa Monica Bay restoration project even though
you were previously involved with the same project in your capac-
ity as a member of the Water Resources Control Board.

As stated previously, this advice is limited to application
of the Act. We suggest that you contact the Attorney General's
office regarding other provisions of law which may apply, such as
Public Contract Code Section 10411.

We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry.
Should you have any further questions regarding this matter, do
not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Blanca M. Breeze
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:BMB:plh


2 "Person" is broadly defined under the Act to include any
organization or group of persons acting in concert. (Section
82047.) Accordingly, Sections 87401 and 87402 restrict your abil-
ity to represent either individuals or other entities in certain
proceedings.
